channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Marcos Reports Only 29% of Local Government Units Achieved Seal of Good Governance in 2023
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. revealed that only 29 percent of Local Government Units (LGUs) in the Philippines managed to pass the Seal of Good Governance for the year 2023. The Seal of Good Governa...

item.title: Atiku urges Tinubu to take immediate action on recurring attacks across Nigeria
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar don yan make President Bola Tinubu suppose stop di big big talk and begin serious work to protect lives after di recent killings wey don scatter for different stat...

item.title: Hamilton Man Revives Typewriter Culture, Encourages Intentional Writing
item.description: Jonathan Marshall, a Hamilton resident and typewriter enthusiast, has opened The Hamilton Typewriter Company, a unique shop and museum dedicated to the preservation and appreciation of typewriters. Af...

item.title: Hungary’s youth turn away from Orban amid growing discontent and emigration fears
item.description: As Hungary faces a decisive election, younger voters who grew up under Prime Minister Viktor Orban are increasingly supporting the opposition, with some considering emigration should Orban secure anot...

item.title: NBI questions completion claim of P92-million Pandi, Bulacan flood control project amid missing structures
item.description: A National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) official disclosed during former Senator Bong Revilla’s malversation case hearing that a flood control project in Pandi, Bulacan was reported as fully complete...

item.title: Ontario municipalities face $4B shortfall as local budgets strain under provincial program costs
item.description: Municipalities across Ontario say they can no longer afford to cover the escalating costs of provincial programs such as social services and health care. The Association of Municipalities of Ontario (...

item.title: Kat Borlongan, Filipina Chief Impact Officer at Contentsquare, Receives Knight of the National Order of Merit from France
item.description: A prominent Filipina technology leader, Kat Borlongan, has been honored by the French government with the prestigious Knight of the National Order of Merit. This recognition highlights her significant...

item.title: Farmers Increasing Productivity and Profits Through Sustainable Practices
item.description: Farming is a significant industry in Australia, contributing an estimated A$100 billion to the economy in 2026. However, the industry has been seen as a major emitter of greenhouse gases and a contrib...

item.title: Cross River Gov inaugurates Homeland Safety offices to strengthen community security
item.description: Cross River State Governor, Senator Bassey Otu, don officially commission the Southern District office of the Homeland Safety and Security Agency for Calabar as part of im plan to boost community-base...

item.title: Closure of the Strait of Hormuz Could Raise Global Food Prices Through Fertilizer Shortages
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz amid the war with Iran has triggered a global energy and supply crisis that now threatens the world’s food system. Around one-third of all internationally traded fe...

item.title: BuCor Requests NBI Investigation into Deadly Palawan Shootout
item.description: The Bureau of Corrections (BuCor) has requested the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) to lead an inquiry into a deadly shooting incident in Puerto Princesa City, Palawan, that resulted in the dea...

item.title: Teenager Dies in Bedfordshire Crash: Couple Charged
item.description: A couple, Jamie Fountain and Ellie Ireland, have appeared in court following the tragic death of a 13-year-old boy, Noah Campbell, in a road collision in Flitwick, Bedfordshire. The incident occurred ...

item.title: Holocaust Museum quietly alters educational materials and cancels democracy workshop after Trump’s return to office
item.description: The United States Holocaust Memorial Museum has quietly changed some of its online content and canceled a civic‑education program in the months following Donald Trump’s return to the presidency, raisi...

item.title: DCI Investigates Suspected Irregularities in Kenya’s Emergency Fuel Procurement
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has ramped up investigations into the emergency fuel procurement by the Ministry of Energy and Petroleum, which is suspected to have bypassed normal pr...

item.title: TS Galaxy reach Nedbank Cup final, aiming for second title
item.description: TS Galaxy have secured their place in the Nedbank Cup final after a tense 4-3 penalty shootout victory over Milford at Solomon Mahlangu Stadium on Sunday. This marks the Rockets' second appearance in ...

item.title: Canada's Economy and Equity Market Resilient One Year After U.S. Tariffs
item.description: One year after the imposition of U.S. tariffs on April 2, 2025, Canada’s economy has demonstrated remarkable resilience. Despite the trade sanctions and their negative impact on employment and manufac...

item.title: India sends Minister Kirti Vardhan Singh to attend swearing-in of Myanmar’s newly elected President Min Aung Hlaing
item.description: India will send its Minister of State for External Affairs, Kirti Vardhan Singh, to represent the country at the inauguration of Myanmar’s newly elected president, Min Aung Hlaing. The ceremony will b...

item.title: Guidelines for a Safe and Inclusive Dog-Friendly Workplace
item.description: Dogs are becoming more common in Australian workplaces, from occasional 'take your dog to work' days to fully pet-friendly offices. Research indicates that having dogs at work can reduce stress and en...

item.title: Lagos Government adds 500 traffic officers to manage movement ahead of President Tinubu’s project inauguration
item.description: Lagos State Government don yan say e go deploy 500 extra LASTMA officers to the main roads and areas wey dey important before President Bola Tinubu visit Lagos on April 8 and 9, 2026. The Commissioner...

item.title: Centre introduces rules to manage tar balls, sets penalties for oil spill pollution
item.description: In a significant move to tackle marine pollution, the Indian government has proposed new rules to manage tar balls, a hazardous by-product of oil spills. These tar balls, which originate from offshore...
